methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $50M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $29.0B.

data.narrative:
Swift Beat, a Series A–stage Defense & Security company founded by Eric Schmidt, is valued against a peer universe of 10 comparable companies: 2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Scale AI, Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, and Ares Industries.

Two valuation methods triangulate Swift Beat's fair value. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the company's $50M total raised as a proxy for post-Series A advancement, implying $100M. Peer-median analysis across four same-sector, same-stage comparables yields $400M, though the peer set exhibits extreme dispersion: a floor of $150M (Ares Industries) and a ceiling of $29.0B (Scale AI). This wide range reflects heterogeneity in business model and capital efficiency within early-stage defense tech.

Triangulating both methods produces a valuation range of $85M to $460M, with a median of $250M. The stage-progression method establishes a conservative floor; the peer median anchors toward the upper bound. The key uncertainty is composition of the peer set—Scale AI's $29B valuation (likely reflecting AI infrastructure rather than pure defense applications) exerts material upward pressure on the median. A narrower peer set excluding extreme outliers would pull the implied value materially lower. Swift Beat's valuation should reflect capital deployed, stage progression mechanics, and defensible position within a heterogeneous peer landscape.
